blocks:
Sūrat al-Qāriʿah

Makkan, and it is 11 verses

Sūrat al-Qāriʿah

Makkan, and it is eleven verses according to the Kūfī and Ḥijāzī counts, and eight according to the Baṣrī and Shāmī counts.

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الْقارِعَةُ ** (1) The Striking Disaster! which strikes the people with terrors, and the celestial bodies (الاجرام) with splitting (الانفطار) and scattering (الانتشار). مَا الْقارِعَةُ ** (2) What is the Striking Disaster? What is it? Meaning: what thing is it? This is for the magnification of its affair and the terrifying nature of it. So the explicit noun (الظاهر) was placed in the position of the pronoun (المضمر) because it is more terrifying for it. Al-Qummi: Allah repeats it due to its terror, and the people are terrified by it.

(٢) وَما أَدْراكَ مَا الْقارِعَةُ ** (2) What will show you what the Calamity is? And what thing informed you what it is? Meaning that you do not know its essence (كنهها), for it is greater than for the knowledge (دراية) of anyone to reach it. (٣) يَوْمَ يَكُونُ النَّاسُ كَالْفَراشِ الْمَبْثُوثِ ** (3) The day the people will be like scattered moths, in their multitude, their abasement (ذلتهم), their dispersal (انتشارهم), and their agitation (اضطرابهم). (٤) وَتَكُونُ الْجِبالُ كَالْعِهْنِ الْمَنْفُوشِ ** (4) and the mountains will be like carded wool. Like colored (ذي الألوان) carded (المندوف) wool, due to the separation of its parts and their scattering (تطايرها) in the atmosphere (الجو). (٥) فَأَمَّا مَنْ ثَقُلَتْ مَوازِينُهُ ** (5) As for him whose scales are heavy, with good deeds (الحسنات), meaning that the measures (مقادير) of the types of his good deeds (حسناته) outweigh [the bad], فَهُوَ فِي عِيشَةٍ (then he will be in a life), meaning in an existence (عيش), راضِيَةٍ ** (pleasing/satisfied), possessing satisfaction (رضى) or being pleasing/satisfactory (مرضية). (٦) وَأَمَّا مَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوازِينُهُ ** (6) But as for him whose scales are light, of good deeds (الحسنات), meaning that he did not have a good deed (حسنة) worth considering (يعبأ بها), or his evil deeds (سيّئاته) outweighed his good deeds (حسناته)—and the verification (تحقيق) of the weighing (الوزن) and the scale (الميزان) has already passed in Surat al-A'raf—فَأُمُّهُ هاوِيَةٌ (then his mother is Hawiyah), so his abode (مأواه) is the Fire. He takes refuge in it just as the child takes refuge in his mother. And al-Hawiyah (الهاوية) is one of the names of the Fire. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: The crown of his head (أمّ رأسه) will be overturned in the Fire upon his head. I say: Meaning, he will fall (يهوى) into it upon the crown of his head (أمّ رأسه).

(٧) وَما أَدْراكَ ما هِيَهْ. (7) And what will show you what it is? (٨) نارٌ حامِيَةٌ ** (8) A scorching fire. Possessing heat (ذات حمى), meaning intensely hot (شديدة الحرارة). في ثواب الأعمال In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: Whoever recites and frequently recites al-Qari'ah, Allah will grant him security from the trial (فتنة) of the Dajjal—that he should believe in him—and from the blast (فيح) of Hell (جهنّم) on the Day of Resurrection. May Allah grant us its recitation, if Allah the Almighty wills.
